title: Developer Host Cutover Preflight And Execution Planning
objective: Define the bounded preflight and execution-planning surface required to make the Olares One the authoritative always-on development workstation while reducing the field laptop to a client-only operator surface.
dependency_note: Packet 095 closed the prior Olares expansion lane as dormant and authorable only with new evidence. The newly stated hard constraint is different: the field laptop cannot remain the always-on workstation, so Olares developer residency becomes a prerequisite for further delivery. This packet is planning-only and does not reopen generic Olares execution or feature delivery.
required_inputs: The published Packet 095 dormancy posture for the prior Olares expansion lane
The hard operational constraint that the field laptop cannot remain the durable development host
The authoritative host path shape under /home/olares/code/apex established during Phase 5
The current Olares post-closure roadmap and checklist surfaces
The new milestone and technical cutover plans authored on 2026-05-05
constrained_outputs: A bounded planning decision that re-sequences Olares work around developer residency rather than generic expansion
An ordered list of the minimum cutover proof slices required before resumed product delivery
A first execution packet recommendation for canonical host residency and toolchain revalidation
Explicit no-go items preserving the current closure around public ingress, AI-services expansion, code-hosting transition, and laptop-first feature delivery
validation_steps: Confirm the packet does not reopen the dormant simultaneous-worker or Phase 5 source/test execution lane.
Confirm the packet treats Olares developer residency as a prerequisite and planning problem, not as implicit implementation approval.
Confirm no public ingress, AI-services expansion, Gitea transition, canonical-hosting transition, source mutation, runtime mutation, or package mutation is authorized by this packet.
Confirm the packet names a separate later execution packet for host residency and toolchain proof rather than collapsing planning and execution into one uncontrolled lane.
Confirm the packet preserves GitHub-canonical publication while moving durable development state to the Olares host.
ordered_cutover_slices: Authoritative host residency and path authority proof
Canonical host toolchain and runtime parity proof
Host-side validation residency proof for active application and shared package lanes
Publication continuity proof without changing GitHub-canonical hosting
explicit_no_go_items: feature delivery from the laptop-first posture
source, package, or runtime mutation not required for cutover proof
next_packet_candidate: Olares Dev Residency 002 - Canonical Host Residency And Toolchain Revalidation
verdict_summary: Olares developer-host cutover is now the controlling prerequisite for resumed product delivery because the field laptop cannot remain the durable always-on development workstation. Packet 002 remains the correct next bounded execution packet and must use the Milestone 1 acceptance checklist as its audit surface.
preserved_closures: source/package/runtime mutation not required for cutover proof
sql_permission_note: Planning only. This packet authorizes no database writes, source execution, runtime mutation, package mutation, public ingress change, code-hosting transition, or old-clone mutation.
closure_note: Executed 2026-05-05 as a planning-only packet. The verdict is affirmative: developer-host cutover is the controlling prerequisite before resumed product delivery, and Packet 002 remains the correct bounded execution lane for canonical host residency and toolchain revalidation against the Milestone 1 checklist.
next_action: Execute Olares Dev Residency 002 against the Milestone 1 acceptance checklist without reopening feature delivery, public ingress, code-hosting transition, the dormant simultaneous-worker lane, or old-clone mutation.
